sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Withdraw treasury funds for Phase 2 of a Cardano-native ticketing platform operated by Sellout.io and built by Anvil Development Agency.
Recipient: Intersect as budget administrator for Anvil/Sellout delivery through the Sundae Labs TRSC/PSSC framework

Deliverables: Secondary ticket marketplace with on-chain settlement, Smart-contract-enforced royalty splits, Per-event anti-scalping controls, Custodial wallet onboarding for Sellout users, Organizer tools and dashboards, Independent third-party security audit, Public launch campaign and trade-show activations, Public metrics dashboard and quarterly reporting
Deadline/expiry: 8 months across five milestone-gated milestones

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Phase 1 of the platform is already live on Cardano mainnet with on-chain event creation, CIP-68 ticket minting, transfers, and attendance verification funded by Sellout with over $130,000 of its own capital.

Claim (economic, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal requests 4,969,231 ada, stated as about $1,093,231 at $0.22 per ADA, inclusive of a 3% Intersect administration fee, over 8 months.

Claim (technical, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): Phase 2 is intended to deliver a secondary marketplace, royalty enforcement, anti-scalping controls, wallet onboarding, organizer tools, an independent audit, and launch amplification.
Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): Sellout has 200,000+ registered users, has issued 250,000+ tickets, and runs 350+ events annually at its current cadence.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The proposal projects 12-month outcomes of 200+ events on-chain, 75,000+ CIP-68 NFT tickets, 150,000+ on-chain interactions, $6,000,000+ ticket revenue on-chain, and 20,000+ new wallets.

Claim (adoption, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The Yellowstone Club 2026 concert series is presented as a contracted anchor deployment projected to move more than $6,000,000 through Cardano in year one.
missing: Independent evidence for: Phase 1 of the platform is already live on Cardano mainnet with on-chain event creation, CIP-68 ticket minting, transfers, and attendance verification funded by Sellout with over $130,000 of its own capital.
Independent evidence for: Sellout has 200,000+ registered users, has issued 250,000+ tickets, and runs 350+ events annually at its current cadence.

Independent evidence for: The proposal projects 12-month outcomes of 200+ events on-chain, 75,000+ CIP-68 NFT tickets, 150,000+ on-chain interactions, $6,000,000+ ticket revenue on-chain, and 20,000+ new wallets.
Independent evidence for: The Yellowstone Club 2026 concert series is presented as a contracted anchor deployment projected to move more than $6,000,000 through Cardano in year one.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: unknown (stale snapshot)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The proposal requests 4,969,231 ada, stated as about $1,093,231 at $0.22 per ADA, inclusive of a 3% Intersect administration fee, over 8 months." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "Phase 1 of the platform is already live on Cardano mainnet with on-chain event creation, CIP-68 ticket minting, transfers, and attendance verification funded by Sellout with over $130,000 of its own capital." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: a treasury action without a complete deep-research dossier cannot be voted directionally without pretending certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.
